title: Isaac Sim trajectory stream (control output) -- PROVISIONAL
description: 성진's control output (control_seoungjin/sample: run_and_log.py writes isaacsim_trajectory.json; CONVENTIONS.md 'Trajectory / motor-command interface'): a time-stamped stream of body poses. Quaternions are wxyz to match common.geometry.quat_wxyz_to_R. PROVISIONAL -- 성진 waits on 윤호 to finalise the Isaac Sim JSON schema; 성진 emits the simplest form and only the conversion logic changes once this is pinned. NOTE ON TIME: on the 성진 control JSON side 'time' is FLOAT SECONDS, NOT integer nanoseconds -- the ns integer clock is the separate vision/VIO stream (CONVENTIONS.md 'Timestamps'); do not conflate the two.

fps.description: Nominal sample rate in frames per second (> 0). Informational: the authoritative time base is each frame's float-seconds 'time'.

frames.description: Ordered stream of pose frames (may be empty).

time.description: Timestamp in FLOAT SECONDS on 성진's control clock (e.g. 0.01), >= 0. NOT integer nanoseconds (that clock is the separate vision/VIO stream, CONVENTIONS.md 'Timestamps').

position.description: Body-origin position [x, y, z] in the +Z-up world frame, metres.

yaw_rad.description: Heading (yaw) in radians about world +Z. 성진's orientation_quat_wxyz is yaw-only; this is the same heading, kept for consumers that only need it.

orientation_quat_wxyz.description: Body orientation as a unit quaternion in WXYZ order [w, x, y, z], world<-body, yaw-only (feeds common.geometry.quat_wxyz_to_R).
